text: left common carotid artery, left subclavian artery, patent ductus arteriosus and the right arch compressing the oesophagus.
We can see all the structures in this photograph.
PDA is ligated, cross clamps are applied to the left arch and left subclavian artery together and part of arch just distal to the patent ductus arteriosus.
So that the perfusion to descending thoracic aorta is maintained by the right arch.
The aorta just distal to the left subclavian artery is divided and PDA is excised.
Both the ends are closed with 6 ohm proline sutures.
Cross clamps are released and perfusion to the lower limbs, upper limbs and carotids checked.
